rulings.Case Title: HECTOR PAZOS VS. CASA THAI MARKET, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Respond To Three Discovery Orders And Request For Additional Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday June 19, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT CASA THAI MARKET, INC., HUNG LI'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Respond To Three Discovery Orders And Request For Additional Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion by the defendant for terminating sanctions is granted. The plaintiff has disregarded three previous discovery orders of this Court. The Court's last order specifically advised failure to respond to the discovery would result in case termination. An order specifying the basis for the termination sanction to be prepared by the defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA L. TANACEA VS. RICHARD R. TAVERNETTI MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 19, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT EXACTECH, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The Fourth Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately alleges that Dr. Tavernetti was acting as Exactech's agent at the time the alleged misrepresentations and/or concealments were made. The FAC pleads specific facts as to how the agency relationship was created and pleads specific facts showing how Exactech exerted control over Dr. Tavernetti at the time the alleged misrepresentations and/or concealments were made. (FAC, pars. 16-16, 77, 88.) Further, the court must accept as true allegations of an agency relationship for purposes of demurrer. (City of Industry v. City of Fillmore (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 191, 212-13.) The FAC also sufficiently pleads all of the elements of fraudulent misrepresentation: (1) Dr. Tavernetti misrepresented that P's only option was hip replacement (FAC, par. 71-72) (2) Dr. Tavernetti knew this misrepresentation was false (FAC, par. 72); (3) Dr. Tavernetti made this misrepresentation to induce P into getting an unnecessary surgery (FAC, par. 72); (4) P reasonably relied on these misrepresentations (FAC, par. 78); and (5) P was damaged as a result of these misrepresentations (FAC, pars. 80-81.) The FAC also sufficiently pleads all of the elements of fraudulent concealment: (1) Dr. Tavernetti concealed his relationship with Exactech (FAC, par. 86), (2) Plaintiff did not know about the concealed facts (FAC, par. 89); (3) Dr. Tavernetti intended to deceive Plaintiff by concealing these facts (FAC, pars. 88-87); (4) Plaintiff would have acted differently had the facts been disclosed (FAC, pars. 89-90); and (5) Plaintiff was harmed and the concealment was a substantial factor in her harm (FAC, pars. 91-92.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA L. TANACEA VS. RICHARD R. TAVERNETTI MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike And Motion To Strike Certain Allegations And Damages Claims In Plaintiffs Fourth Amended Complaint By Defendant Exactech, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 19, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT EXACTECH, INC.'S Motion To Strike Certain Allegations And Damages Claims In Plaintiffs Fourth Amended Complaint. Denied. As set forth in the ruling on demurrer, the Fourth Amended Complaint adequately pleads causes of action for fraudulent misrepresentation and fraudulent concealment against Defendant. The fraud allegations support a claim for punitive damages. rulings.Case Title: CLARENCE WILSON et al VS. 1049 MARKET STREET, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Clarence Wilson, Torrance Antoni, Michael Mason, And Steban Guavara To Appear For A Mental Health Examination
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday June 19, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT 1049 MARKET STREET, LLC'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Clarence Wilson, Torrance Antoni, Michael Mason, And Steban Guavara To Appear For A Mental Health Examination. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion for mental health examinations of plaintiffs is untimely and therefore denied. The defendant's notice to set the mental health examinations, served May 18, 2015, sought to schedule same outside the statutory thirty-days-before-trial statutory close of discovery. The plaintiffs timely objected to the notice on that ground and because C.C.P. 2032.310 (a) requires a mental examination to be obtained by leave of court. Discovery closes on June 22, 2015. The motion to set the examination is heard prior to the discovery cut-off, but requests the mental examination dates to take place outside the cut-off date. The defendant urges its "meet and confer" efforts are a sufficient reason to set the examinations after the discovery cut-off. "Meet and confer" efforts are critical to the discovery process, but that they were unsuccessful is not a sufficient reason for the delay in seeking the relief sought to occur during the permissible open discovery time frame. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

And Betty Kaplan's Motion To Compel Additional Responses To Special Interrogatories, Requests For Admission, And Form Interrogatories, Set One, Propounded By Roy Toliver To Defendants Laleh Zelinsky, David And Betty Kaplan, Asm Investments, And Turk Bd LLC. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court intends to grant the motion to compel. The defendants shall explain why the discovery sought is not within their knowledge based on the fact a co-defendant was the building manager and both are represented by the same counsel. The May 11, 2015 "meet and confer" email will be a subject of the hearing to establish the scope of the discovery compliance order to be issued. rulings.Case Title: 1016 WASHINGTON STREET, LLC VS. SUZANNE MEREDITH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Deposition Subpoena/ Alt For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday June 19, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT SUZANNE MEREDITH'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena or in the Alternative For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to quash is granted. The defendant's bank records are constitutionally protected based on her right of privacy. See Harris vs. Superior Court (1992) 3 Cal. App 4th 661. The specific language of the subpoena issued for the bank records is overbroad and in its present form meets the definition of a "fishing expedition". The plaintiff has not made a sufficient showing of necessity and inability to obtain the constitutionally protected information by other means through its reference to earlier focused discovery requests to which objections have been interposed. The moving party's request for sanctions, not having been initially raised in the Notice of Motion, is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
